page: Microsoft Azure F32als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache per core. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the F32als_v7 demonstrates strong CPU and memory performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates rank in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and the PassMark CPU score fall within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only small block, read-only large block, and write large block, consistently place in the top 25%. Database operations under PassMark rank in the top 10%, while Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference speeds for small (135M) and medium (7B) models reach the top 10% and top 25% respectively, though performance drops to average (middle 50%) for large (70B) models.

The F32als_v7 offers high compute density and strong single-core performance, making it a cost-efficient option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. However, the low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU and the complete absence of local storage represent significant tradeoffs, requiring external network storage attachment for data-intensive operations. This server type is suited for compute-heavy tasks such as web serving, batch processing, compression utilities, database operations, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F32als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv7 family, featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it is configured with 64.0 GB of memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, high-speed data compression, and strong memory bandwidth. While cost-efficient for compute-dense workloads, its low memory allocation and lack of local storage present clear tradeoffs. It is suited for web serving, database operations, and small-to-medium scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F32als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high single-core performance for memory-lean, CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz.
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and data compression speeds.
No local storage included, with support for external Premium Storage.
Suited for web serving, database operations, and small-scale LLM inference.
